concept_name: Magnetic Field of a Long Solenoid (B = μ₀nI inside, ≈0 outside)
source_book: DC Pandey Vol 2 Ch.26 §26.8 (Magnetic field due to a long solenoid) — table-of-contents reference only; teaching authored from first principles. Diamond #4 of the magnetism chapter (M4 binary-gate validator). The atomic concept being taught is the *direction* (axial inside, near-zero outside; right-hand rule grip swap from wire-RHR) and *magnitude* (B = μ₀nI, with n = turns per unit length). PRIMARY aha lives in STATE_4: each current element's radial field is annihilated by its diametric twin across the ring (STATE_3) so only the axial components survive and ADD into one uniform axial field (STATE_4) — what looks like chaotic overlapping circles becomes one uniform axial field. SUPPORTING aha in STATE_6: the right-hand grip swaps roles — for a straight wire thumb=I, fingers=B, but for a solenoid fingers wrap WITH the current loops and thumb gives B inside. Four most common misconceptions targeted: B is radial (looks like spokes), outside is just a weaker version of inside, total turns N matters (not density n), and the wire-RHR transfers unchanged to solenoids. Conceptual-only ship per MAGNETISM_ARCHITECTURE.md M1–M6 carve-out; board mode (M7) and competitive mode (M8) deferred. Diamond #4 is the binary-gate validator for the recursive-bootstrap method: templated from Diamond #1 magnetic_field_wire, must ship with ≤1 round of human iteration to prove the architect→physics_author→json_author→quality_auditor pipeline. Rule 31 retrofit 2026-07-04: converted to straightforward distinct-motion beats with per-state contextual controls — no prediction pauses, glow bindings drive focus, every state's script cut to ≤3 tight sentences (~18-20s real narration; founder pacing feedback same day), STATE_5 exposes I only (crank I: inside brightens, outside stays ≈0), STATE_7 exposes n only (the n slider PHYSICALLY rewinds the coil — solSetVisualTurns swaps the winding to a visibly denser/looser helix and the flow dots ride it — plus interior brightness + readout), STATE_8 gained a mid-state battery reversal (scenario_cue battery_reverse; dots + arrows + poles flip together, watched happening), STATE_9 is the open explorer (all controls, duration 0). Comprehension keystone added same pass: 6-question assessment + coverage_map + per-state misconception_watch.

real_world_anchor.primary: Picture the MRI machine in a Bangalore hospital — the tube the patient slides into. That tube is a giant solenoid, kilometres of copper wire coiled tightly around the patient's bore. When current flows through those coils, the inside of the tube fills with a powerful, uniform magnetic field — 1.5 tesla, ten thousand times stronger than Earth's field. The patient's body sits in that uniform field, and hydrogen atoms inside their tissues respond. The whole imaging trick depends on one piece of physics: the field inside a long solenoid is UNIFORM and AXIAL. If the field varied from point to point inside the tube, the image would be unreadable. The MRI works because B = μ₀nI is constant everywhere inside.
real_world_anchor.secondary: Picture the starter motor in a Mumbai auto-rickshaw. When the driver turns the key, current surges through a small solenoid coil; the resulting axial field yanks an iron core inward, which mechanically pushes the starter pinion into the engine flywheel. No solenoid → no engine start. Every auto-rickshaw on Indian roads carries this concept under the hood.
real_world_anchor.tertiary: Picture a doorbell button in a Chennai apartment. Press it and a small solenoid coil inside the bell housing energizes; the resulting axial field pulls an iron plunger that strikes the chime. Release the button, the field collapses, a spring snaps the plunger back. Solenoids turn an electrical pulse into a mechanical kick — the same principle as the MRI's giant uniform field, scaled down to a single chime strike.

N_demo.name: Total turn count (display only — for STATE_7 stretch demonstrative)

L_demo.name: Solenoid length (display only — paired with N_demo to show n = N/L)

formulas.n_definition: n = N / L (turns per unit length, units /m)
formulas.axial_superposition: B_axial_total = sum of B_axial from each turn — radial components cancel between adjacent turns; only axial components survive
formulas.B_outside_long_limit: B_outside ≈ 0 (contributions from turns at different axial positions cancel almost everywhere outside)
formulas.rhr_solenoid: Right-hand grip — fingers curl WITH the current loops, thumb gives B inside (role-swap from wire-RHR where thumb is current)
formulas.reverse_current: Reversing current direction reverses B_inside direction; magnitude |B| = μ₀·n·|I| is unchanged
formulas.stretch_demonstrative: If N stays fixed and L doubles, n halves, B halves

constraints: Long-solenoid idealization: L >> R (length much greater than radius of one turn). Formula B = μ₀·n·I is exact only in this limit.
End effects ignored: at the open ends, B weakens to roughly μ₀·n·I/2; we do not model end-fringing in the renderer.
B_outside treated as 0 in renderer though physically it is vanishingly small (cancellation argument).
Current is uniform around every turn — the same I flows through the entire coiled wire.
Direction by right-hand-rule curl: fingers along current loops, thumb along B inside.
Field is purely axial inside (zero radial, zero azimuthal component) in the long-solenoid limit.

aha_moment.statement: Axial pieces add into one uniform field; radial pieces cancel.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: The two blue axial halves stack head-to-tail into a doubled arrow and the interior fills with uniform axial field lines while the per-turn circles fade to 30%.

STATE_1.title: The Coiled Wire — What if we wrap a current?

scene_composition.text: Straight wire → coiled wire

Same wire. Same current.
Different shape — different field.

Watch the wire bend
into a spring (helix).

tts_sentences.text_en: You already know the field around a single wire — circles.
tts_sentences.text_hi: आप पहले से जानते हैं कि एक single wire के आसपास field कैसा होता है — circles।
tts_sentences.text_te: మీకు ఒక single wire చుట్టూ field ఎలా ఉంటుందో ఇప్పటికే తెలుసు — circles.

tts_sentences.text_en: But what if we coil the wire — wrap it into a spring shape?
tts_sentences.text_hi: लेकिन क्या होगा अगर हम wire को coil करें — उसे एक spring shape में wrap करें?
tts_sentences.text_te: కానీ మనం wire ని coil చేస్తే ఏమవుతుంది — దాన్ని spring shape లో wrap చేస్తే?

tts_sentences.text_en: Same wire, same current — but the combined field changes shape dramatically. Watch.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Same wire, same current — लेकिन combined field का shape dramatically बदल जाता है। देखिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: Same wire, same current — కానీ combined field యొక్క shape dramatically మారుతుంది. చూడండి.

misconception_watch.belief: coiling the wire makes it a new device with new physics
misconception_watch.visual_counter: the SAME straight wire visibly bends into the helix with the same current dots still flowing through it
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: same wire, same current — only the geometry changed, so only the field's SHAPE changes.

STATE_2.title: Each Turn Makes Its Own Field

scene_composition.text: One turn at a time — same B-circles as a straight wire

Reminder: a straight wire
makes B-circles around it
(covered in Diamond #1).

Six turns. Six sets of circles.
The chaos hides a pattern.

tts_sentences.text_en: Each turn is just the straight wire bent into a circle — the same B-circles wrap right around it.
tts_sentences.text_hi: हर turn बस एक circle में मुड़ी हुई straight wire है — वही B-circles उसके चारों ओर wrap होते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ప్రతి turn అనేది circle లో వంగిన straight wire మాత్రమే — అవే B-circles దాని చుట్టూ wrap అవుతాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: And each turn's net push points straight along the axis — watch the little blue arrows appear turn by turn.
tts_sentences.text_hi: और हर turn का net push सीधे axis के साथ point करता है — छोटे blue arrows को turn by turn आते हुए देखिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: మరియు ప్రతి turn యొక్క net push నేరుగా axis వెంట point చేస్తుంది — చిన్న blue arrows turn by turn రావడం చూడండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Six turns look chaotic — but the chaos hides a pattern: the radial parts will cancel, only the axial parts survive.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Six turns chaotic लगते हैं — लेकिन इस chaos में एक pattern छिपा है: radial parts cancel हो जाएंगे, केवल axial parts survive करेंगे।
tts_sentences.text_te: Six turns chaotic గా కనిపిస్తాయి — కానీ ఈ chaos లో ఒక pattern దాగి ఉంది: radial parts cancel అవుతాయి, axial parts మాత్రమే survive చేస్తాయి.

STATE_3.title: Radial Pieces Cancel — Every Twin Across the Ring

scene_composition.text: Radial pieces cancel — net sideways field is zero

Every bit of current has a
twin directly across the ring.
Their sideways fields are
equal and OPPOSITE.

Split each twin's field:
one piece along the axis,
one pointing sideways (radial).
Same trick as a force on an incline.

tts_sentences.text_en: Pick a point on the axis, and look at one tiny piece of current — and its twin directly across the ring.
tts_sentences.text_hi: axis पर एक point चुनें, और current का एक tiny टुकड़ा देखें — और ring के उस पार उसका twin।
tts_sentences.text_te: axis పై ఒక point ఎంచుకోండి, current యొక్క ఒక tiny భాగాన్ని చూడండి — మరియు ring కి అవతల నేరుగా దాని twin ని.

tts_sentences.text_en: Split each twin's field into an axial piece and a sideways piece. The two sideways pieces are equal and opposite — they annihilate.
tts_sentences.text_hi: हर twin की field को एक axial piece और एक sideways piece में split करें। दोनों sideways pieces equal और opposite हैं — वे annihilate हो जाते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ప్రతి twin యొక్క field ని ఒక axial piece మరియు ఒక sideways piece గా split చేయండి. రెండు sideways pieces equal మరియు opposite — అవి annihilate అవుతాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Sum every twin and every ring — only the along-axis pieces survive.
tts_sentences.text_hi: हर twin और हर ring को sum करें — केवल along-axis pieces बचती हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ప్రతి twin ని, ప్రతి ring ని sum చేయండి — along-axis pieces మాత్రమే మిగులుతాయి.

misconception_watch.belief: B inside points radially outward from the axis, like spokes of a wheel
misconception_watch.visual_counter: the two red radial halves visibly shrink into each other and vanish (annihilate) — net radial exactly zero
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: every radial piece has an equal-opposite twin across the ring; the sideways field cannot survive the sum.

STATE_4.title: Axial Pieces Add — One Uniform Field Inside

scene_composition.text: Axial pieces ADD → one uniform field inside

Both axial pieces point the
SAME way. Stack them head
to tail — they double, not cancel.

Over every twin and every ring:
one steady axial field filling
the inside — like a bar magnet.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now the axial halves — both twins' pieces point the SAME way. Stack them head to tail: they add, they double.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब axial halves — दोनों twins के pieces SAME direction में point करते हैं। उन्हें head to tail stack करें: वे add होते हैं, double हो जाते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు axial halves — రెండు twins యొక్క pieces SAME direction లో point చేస్తాయి. వాటిని head to tail stack చేయండి: అవి add అవుతాయి, double అవుతాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Repeat for every ring along the length — each one adds its axial bit, building the field end to end.
tts_sentences.text_hi: length के साथ हर ring के लिए repeat करें — हर एक अपना axial bit add करती है, field को end to end build करती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: length వెంట ప్రతి ring కోసం repeat చేయండి — ప్రతి ఒక్కటి తన axial bit ని add చేస్తూ, field ని end to end build చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: The result: one uniform axial field filling the inside — exactly what the MRI depends on.
tts_sentences.text_hi: परिणाम: एक uniform axial field जो inside को fill करती है — exactly वही जिस पर MRI निर्भर करती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఫలితం: ఒక uniform axial field inside ని fill చేస్తోంది — MRI సరిగ్గా దేనిపై ఆధారపడుతుందో అదే.

misconception_watch.belief: overlapping fields from many turns must smear into a mess, not a clean uniform field
misconception_watch.visual_counter: the two blue axial halves stack head-to-tail into a doubled arrow, then every ring along L adds its bit — the interior fills with parallel axial lines
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: axial pieces all point the SAME way, so superposition builds order out of the chaos: one uniform axial B.

STATE_5.title: Outside the Solenoid — Where Did the Field Go?

scene_composition.text: Outside the solenoid: B ≈ 0

Inside: uniform, strong, axial
(field is concentrated here)

Outside: turns at different
positions cancel almost
perfectly — B fades to ≈ 0
Switch it on and hang it freely — it lines up North–South. A solenoid is an electromagnet: a bar magnet you control.

tts_sentences.text_en: Most students guess the field outside is just a weaker version of inside. Watch what really happens.
tts_sentences.text_hi: ज़्यादातर students अनुमान लगाते हैं कि outside का field बस inside का एक weaker version है। देखें क्या वास्तव में होता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: చాలామంది students outside field అనేది inside యొక్క weaker version అని అంచనా వేస్తారు. నిజంగా ఏమి జరుగుతుందో చూడండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Outside, the turns' contributions cancel almost completely — the magnetic field B outside is approximately zero.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Outside, turns की contributions लगभग completely cancel हो जाती हैं — magnetic field B outside approximately zero होती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Outside, turns యొక్క contributions దాదాపు completely cancel అవుతాయి — magnetic field B outside approximately zero.

tts_sentences.text_en: All the field stays INSIDE — hang the coil freely and it points North–South: a bar magnet you can switch on and off.
tts_sentences.text_hi: सारी field INSIDE रहती है — coil को freely लटकाएं और यह North–South point करेगी: एक bar magnet जिसे आप switch on और off कर सकते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: మొత్తం field INSIDE లోనే ఉంటుంది — coil ని freely వేలాడదీస్తే అది North–South వైపు point చేస్తుంది: మీరు switch on, off చేయగలిగే bar magnet.

misconception_watch.belief: outside the solenoid B is just a weaker version of inside, falling off gradually like a wire's 1/r field
misconception_watch.visual_counter: the interior is packed with bright axial lines while the few faint external lines loop far around — and with the I slider live, cranking I brightens the INSIDE only; outside stays dead
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: outside, turns at different positions cancel almost perfectly: B outside ≈ 0, not merely weaker.

STATE_6.title: RHR for a Solenoid — Curl with the Current, Thumb is B

scene_composition.text: Same right hand — grip swaps roles

Wire (Diamond #1):
thumb = current I
fingers = field B

Solenoid (now):
fingers = current I
thumb = field B inside
Wire is straight → thumb is straight (= I). Coil curls → fingers curl (= I). The other one is always B.

tts_sentences.text_en: For a straight wire you learned: thumb along the current, fingers curl with the magnetic field B.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Straight wire के लिए आपने सीखा: thumb current के साथ, fingers magnetic field B के साथ curl होती हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: Straight wire కోసం మీరు నేర్చుకున్నారు: thumb current వెంట, fingers magnetic field B తో curl అవుతాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: For a solenoid the roles swap. FINGERS curl with the current loops; THUMB gives the magnetic field B inside.
tts_sentences.text_hi: solenoid के लिए roles swap होते हैं। FINGERS current loops के साथ curl होती हैं; THUMB magnetic field B inside देती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: solenoid కోసం roles swap అవుతాయి. FINGERS current loops తో curl అవుతాయి; THUMB magnetic field B inside ఇస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Same right hand, different grip. Wire: thumb is current. Solenoid: fingers are current — the other one is always B.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Same right hand, different grip। Wire: thumb current है। Solenoid: fingers current हैं — दूसरा हमेशा B होता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Same right hand, different grip. Wire: thumb current. Solenoid: fingers current — మిగిలింది ఎప్పుడూ B.

misconception_watch.belief: the wire right-hand rule transfers unchanged to a solenoid (thumb = current, fingers = B)
misconception_watch.visual_counter: the on-screen hand re-grips: fingers visibly curl WITH the coil's current loops and the thumb lands along the axial B
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: the grip swaps roles — the straight part of the hand matches the straight thing: wire → thumb = I; coil → fingers = I, thumb = B.

STATE_7.title: The Formula — B = μ₀nI, and n is Turns Per Unit Length

scene_composition.text: B = μ₀ n I — three factors, no distance r

n = N / L
(turns per metre, units /m)
DENSITY matters, not raw count.

Double n → double B
Double I → double B
Stretch L 2× (N fixed) → B halves
No 1/r dependence inside — that is what 'uniform' means. B is the same at every interior point.

tts_sentences.text_en: n is turns per unit length — turns N divided by length L. Density matters, not the raw count.
tts_sentences.text_hi: n है turns per unit length — turns N को length L से divide करें। Density matter करती है, raw count नहीं।
tts_sentences.text_te: n అంటే turns per unit length — turns N ని length L తో divide చేయండి. Density matter అవుతుంది, raw count కాదు.

tts_sentences.text_en: The magnetic field B equals mu-naught times n times I. Three factors, no distance r — that is what 'uniform' means.
tts_sentences.text_hi: magnetic field B equals mu-naught times n times I। तीन factors, कोई distance r नहीं — यही 'uniform' का मतलब है।
tts_sentences.text_te: magnetic field B equals mu-naught times n times I. మూడు factors, distance r లేదు — అదే 'uniform' అంటే అర్థం.

tts_sentences.text_en: Slide n and watch the coil itself rewind — pack the turns denser and the magnetic field B doubles with the density.
tts_sentences.text_hi: n को slide करें और coil को खुद rewind होते देखें — turns को denser pack करें और magnetic field B density के साथ double हो जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: n ని slide చేసి coil నే rewind అవ్వడం చూడండి — turns ని denser గా pack చేస్తే magnetic field B density తో పాటు double అవుతుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: the total turn count N alone sets the field — solenoid length does not matter
misconception_watch.visual_counter: the live n slider PHYSICALLY rewinds the coil: lower turns-per-metre → a visibly looser winding, dimmer interior, and the B readout halves
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: only the DENSITY n = N/L enters B = μ₀nI — stretch the same N over twice the length and B halves.

STATE_8.title: Reverse the Current — Field Flips, Magnitude Unchanged

scene_composition.text: Reverse the current → B flips, and the N and S poles swap ends. Magnitude unchanged.

Current direction reverses.
Watch the yellow dots
run the other way.

B inside flips by 180°.
|B| = μ₀ n I unchanged —
only direction reverses.
Right hand again: flip your grip, thumb now points the opposite way. Formula B = μ₀nI doesn't care about sign.

tts_sentences.text_en: The battery is about to reverse. Keep your eye on the yellow current dots.
tts_sentences.text_hi: battery अब reverse होने वाली है। yellow current dots पर नज़र रखें।
tts_sentences.text_te: battery ఇప్పుడు reverse అవ్వబోతోంది. yellow current dots పై దృష్టి పెట్టండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: There — the current reverses, the magnetic field B flips, and the North and South poles swap ends.
tts_sentences.text_hi: वहाँ — current reverse हो गई, magnetic field B flip हो गई, और North और South poles ने ends swap कर लिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: అదిగో — current reverse అయింది, magnetic field B flip అయింది, మరియు North, South poles ends swap చేసుకున్నాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: The magnitude never changed — the magnetic field B = mu-naught n I is blind to direction. Only the sign flipped.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Magnitude कभी नहीं बदली — magnetic field B equals mu-naught n I direction से blind है। केवल sign flip हुआ।
tts_sentences.text_te: Magnitude ఎప్పుడూ మారలేదు — magnetic field B equals mu-naught n I direction కి blind. Sign మాత్రమే flip అయింది.

misconception_watch.belief: reversing the current also changes the field's strength
misconception_watch.visual_counter: the dots, interior arrows, and N/S poles all flip together mid-state while the B readout value stays identical
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: sign flips, magnitude doesn't — |B| = μ₀n|I| is blind to direction.

STATE_9.title: Explore — Tune n, I, and Watch B Inside

scene_composition.text: You drive: tune n and I, watch B = μ₀ n I

Top-right sliders:
n = turns / metre
I = current (A)
B reads live below.

Move the probe INSIDE —
B reads the same anywhere.
Move it OUTSIDE — B ≈ 0.
Do the mental math first: double n in your head — does the B readout match?

tts_sentences.text_en: Now you drive. Crank up the turns per length n — watch the magnetic field B grow proportionally.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब आप drive करें। turns per length n को crank up करें — magnetic field B को proportionally grow होते हुए देखें।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మీరు drive చేయండి. turns per length n ని crank up చేయండి — magnetic field B proportionally grow అవుతున్నది చూడండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Bring the current I down — watch the magnetic field B shrink. Work out the magnetic field B in your head before you read the meter.
tts_sentences.text_hi: current I को नीचे लाएं — magnetic field B को shrink होते हुए देखें। meter पढ़ने से पहले magnetic field B को अपने मन में calculate करें।
tts_sentences.text_te: current I ని తగ్గించండి — magnetic field B shrink అవుతున్నది చూడండి. meter చదవడానికి ముందు magnetic field B ని మీ మనసులో calculate చేయండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Wherever the probe sits INSIDE, the magnetic field B reads the same — that is uniformity. Outside it reads near-zero.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Probe INSIDE कहीं भी हो, magnetic field B same पढ़ता है — यही uniformity है। Outside यह near-zero पढ़ता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Probe INSIDE ఎక్కడ ఉన్నా, magnetic field B same చదువుతుంది — అదే uniformity. Outside అది near-zero చదువుతుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: B inside must weaken away from the coil wall, like 1/r from a wire
misconception_watch.visual_counter: the interior lines are equally bright everywhere between the walls, and the readout B = μ₀nI has no r in it
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: inside a long solenoid B is the SAME at every interior point — no distance dependence at all.

epic_c_branches.misconception: The B-field inside a solenoid points radially outward from the axis, like a star pattern, because each turn creates a circle so the overlap must spread outward.
trigger_phrases: B points outward from the axis
field is radial inside the solenoid
B points away from the centerline

STATE_1.title: Myth: B points radially outward like spokes of a wheel

scene_composition.text: WRONG belief — B points radially outward

Red arrows (wrong):
spokes pointing outward
from the axis

If B were radial, the
MRI image would have no
uniform axis — useless.

tts_sentences.text_en: Some students see the overlapping circles and imagine the field shoots radially outward from the axis, like spokes of a wheel.
Hold this picture for a moment — then we test it against what the field really does.

STATE_2.title: Correction: radial parts CANCEL, axial parts ADD

scene_composition.text: Mirror-image symmetry: radial parts cancel between turns

Each turn's radial part has
an equal-opposite from the
next turn — net radial = 0.

Axial parts all point the
same way (same RHR sense)
→ they ADD up.

tts_sentences.text_en: Two adjacent turns are mirror images across the gap between them. Their radial fields point in opposite directions and cancel.
The axial parts, in contrast, both point the same way — they survive and sum up to one uniform axial B.
So inside the solenoid, B is along the axis — never radial.

epic_c_branches.misconception: The B-field outside the solenoid is just slightly weaker than inside — it falls off gradually like a wire's 1/r field.
trigger_phrases: outside is just weaker than inside
field outside is similar to inside

STATE_1.title: Myth: outside is just a weak version of inside

scene_composition.text: WRONG belief — outside is a weaker version of inside

Red arrows (wrong):
outside has moderate B,
falling off gradually like 1/r.

Sounds reasonable —
a wire's field falls as 1/r.
But a solenoid is different.

tts_sentences.text_en: Many students assume B outside is like B inside, just weaker — falling off gradually with distance.
The intuition comes from the straight wire, where B drops as 1 over r. But a solenoid is not one wire.

scene_composition.text: Outside: turns at different positions cancel — B ≈ 0

Outside any one turn,
far-away turns contribute
fields pointing every way.

Vector sum ≈ 0 everywhere
outside a long solenoid.
All the field stays INSIDE.

tts_sentences.text_en: Outside the solenoid, contributions from turns at different axial positions point in many directions and cancel almost completely.
For a long solenoid, B outside is approximately zero — not just weaker, but essentially gone.
This is why solenoids make excellent electromagnets: all the field energy stays inside the tube.

epic_c_branches.misconception: Doubling the number of turns N doubles the field. Length of the solenoid doesn't matter — only how many turns you have.
trigger_phrases: B depends on total turns N
only N matters in the formula

STATE_1.title: Myth: same N = same B, length doesn't matter

scene_composition.text: WRONG belief — only N (total turns) matters

Two solenoids:
both have N = 10 turns,
one is 2× the length.

Instinct says:
same N → same B.
Let's test that.

tts_sentences.text_en: Picture two solenoids: both have the same total turns N, but one is stretched to twice the length.
Many students assume B is the same in both — same N, same current, same field.

STATE_2.title: Correction: n = N / L is what matters, not N alone

scene_composition.text: Density n = N / L is what enters the formula

Short one: N = 10, L = 0.5 m
n = 20 turns / m
B = μ₀ · 20 · I

Long one: N = 10, L = 1.0 m
n = 10 turns / m
B = HALF of the short one

tts_sentences.text_en: Stretching the solenoid halves the turn density n. With N fixed and L doubled, n halves.
Since B = mu-naught times n times I, B in the longer solenoid is HALF of B in the shorter one.
Total turns N matters only through n. Length matters. Density is the right number.

epic_c_branches.misconception: Use the same right-hand rule as for a wire: thumb along the current, fingers curl in B-direction. That should work for the solenoid too.
trigger_phrases: use the wire right-hand rule for solenoid
same RHR as a straight wire

STATE_1.title: Myth: wire-RHR transfers unchanged to a solenoid

scene_composition.text: WRONG belief — wire-RHR works unchanged for solenoid

Wire-RHR:
thumb = current
fingers = B
(applied to one turn's tangent)

Result: B comes out
radially outward from
the coil — clearly wrong.

tts_sentences.text_en: Some students apply the wire right-hand rule to a single turn's local tangent and try to read B for the whole solenoid.
Thumb along the local current, fingers curl to B — but this gives a radial direction, not the axial answer we know is right.

STATE_2.title: Correction: grip swaps — fingers curl with the coils, thumb is B

scene_composition.text: Solenoid grip: fingers curl WITH the loops, thumb gives B inside

Wire is straight →
thumb is straight = I.
Coil curls →
fingers curl = I.

Whatever isn't I is B.
For a solenoid:
thumb = B (axial inside).

tts_sentences.text_en: For a solenoid, the grip swaps roles. FINGERS curl in the same direction the current flows around the coil.
THUMB then points along B inside the solenoid — axial.
Same right hand, different grip. Wire: thumb = I. Solenoid: fingers = I. The other one is always B.

regeneration_variants.label: Higher current and density
regeneration_variants.scene_hint: Push n = 3000/m and I = 5 A — feel the formula scale.

regeneration_variants.label: Loose coil (low n)
regeneration_variants.scene_hint: Drop n to 200/m — B inside shrinks proportionally.

regeneration_variants.label: Same wire, stretched 2× length
regeneration_variants.physical_world: Take a 500-turn solenoid of 0.5 m and stretch it to 1.0 m — n halves, B halves.
regeneration_variants.same_physics: B = μ₀nI applies to both lengths; only n changes.

STATE_1.label: The Coiled Wire — straight wire morphs into a coil
STATE_1.caption: A straight wire (Diamond #1) coils into a 6-turn helix, the current already flowing through it. No field shown yet — we'll see what the coil does to it.

STATE_2.label: Each turn makes its own B — circles wrap around the wire
STATE_2.caption: Each turn is a wire bent into a circle. The blue B-circles wrap right around the wire; each turn's net field points along the axis.

STATE_3.label: Radial pieces cancel — diametric twins across the ring
STATE_3.caption: On the axis, the field from one current element and its diametric twin split into axial (blue) + radial (red) pieces. The two red radial halves are equal and opposite — they annihilate. Net radial: zero.

STATE_4.label: Axial pieces add — one uniform field survives
STATE_4.caption: The two blue axial halves point the same way — stack them head to tail and they double. Over every twin and every ring: one uniform axial field fills the inside.

STATE_5.label: Outside ≈ 0 — long-solenoid limit
STATE_5.caption: Inside: uniform, strong, axial. The few faint external lines loop right around back to the other end — for a long solenoid B outside falls to ≈ 0. Crank the I slider: the INSIDE brightens, outside stays dead.

STATE_6.label: RHR-swap — fingers curl with current, thumb is B
STATE_6.caption: Right hand again — but the grip swaps. Fingers curl with the coil's current; thumb points along B inside.

STATE_7.label: Formula reveal — B = μ₀ n I, n is turns per metre
STATE_7.caption: B = μ₀ · n · I, where n = N/L is turns per metre. Three factors, no distance r inside. Slide n: the coil PHYSICALLY rewinds — more turns per metre packs the winding denser — and B = μ₀nI tracks it live.

STATE_8.label: Reverse current — B flips, |B| unchanged
STATE_8.caption: The battery reverses MID-STATE: the dots run the other way, the field arrows flip 180°, and N/S swap ends — all in one moment, watched happening. Magnitude |B| = μ₀nI unchanged.

STATE_9.label: Explore — tune I, watch B = μ₀ n I
STATE_9.caption: Drag n and I. The n slider physically rewinds the coil (denser or looser winding); B = μ₀nI tracks both live in the readout and the interior brightness.
STATE_9.formula_overlay:
B = μ₀ n I
n = N / L
Inside: uniform
Outside: ≈ 0

assessment.mastery_definition: A student has mastered the long solenoid's field when they can state that each turn contributes a wire-like field whose RADIAL pieces cancel in diametric-twin pairs while the AXIAL pieces all add, giving one uniform axial field B = μ₀nI inside (no distance dependence at any interior point); that outside a long solenoid contributions from turns at different positions cancel almost completely so B ≈ 0 (not merely weaker); that n is turns PER UNIT LENGTH (n = N/L) so stretching the same N over twice the length halves B; that the solenoid grip rule swaps roles versus the wire rule (fingers curl with the current loops, thumb gives B inside); and that reversing the current flips B's direction and swaps the N/S pole ends while leaving |B| = μ₀n|I| unchanged. (End-fringing and the toroid are downstream refinements, not assessed by this conceptual V1 quiz.)

questions.stem: At a point on the solenoid's axis, consider one tiny current element on a turn and its twin directly across the ring. What do their sideways (radial) field pieces do?

options.A: They are equal and opposite, so they cancel exactly
options.B: They add, making B point radially outward like spokes
options.C: They combine into a swirl circling the axis
options.D: Nothing — each element's field is already purely axial

distractor_misconceptions.B: Holds the radial-spokes picture of the solenoid interior
distractor_misconceptions.C: Confuses the azimuthal field AROUND a wire with the resultant ON the axis
distractor_misconceptions.D: Skips the vector decomposition — individual elements DO have radial pieces; only the SUM is axial

questions.tested_idea: Diametric twins annihilate the radial components on the axis
questions.parallel_form_stem: Two current elements sit at opposite ends of one turn's diameter. At the centre of the turn, how do their radial field contributions combine?

questions.stem: Why is the field inside a long solenoid uniform and directed along the axis?

options.A: The axial pieces from every twin and every ring point the same way and add, while all radial pieces cancel
options.B: The metal of the coil channels the field along the tube
options.C: Only the nearest turn contributes, and its field happens to be axial
options.D: Overlapping fields always average out into a uniform field

distractor_misconceptions.B: Attributes the field shape to the material instead of superposition
distractor_misconceptions.C: Ignores superposition — every turn contributes everywhere inside
distractor_misconceptions.D: Treats uniformity as automatic rather than a consequence of the axial-add / radial-cancel structure

questions.tested_idea: Axial superposition builds ONE uniform field (THE AHA)
questions.parallel_form_stem: Inside a long solenoid, what happens to the axial field pieces contributed by the many turns stacked along its length?

questions.stem: For a long solenoid carrying current, the field OUTSIDE the coil is best described as:

options.A: Approximately zero — contributions from turns at different positions cancel almost completely
options.B: A weaker version of the inside field, falling off gradually like 1/r
options.C: The same strength as inside, but pointing the other way
options.D: Strongest right at the wires and still strong far away

distractor_misconceptions.B: Transfers the straight wire's 1/r falloff to the solenoid
distractor_misconceptions.C: Misreads the return flux as a full-strength reversed field
distractor_misconceptions.D: Confuses the near-wire local field with the net outside field

questions.tested_idea: B outside a long solenoid ≈ 0 by cancellation
questions.parallel_form_stem: You crank the current in a long solenoid to five times its value. What happens to the field just outside the middle of the coil?

questions.stem: Which right-hand grip gives the direction of B inside a solenoid?

options.A: Fingers curl in the direction the current flows around the loops; the thumb points along B inside
options.B: Thumb along the current, fingers curl along B — same as for a straight wire
options.C: Use the left hand, since the coil reverses the geometry
options.D: Fingers point along B outside; the thumb gives the current

distractor_misconceptions.B: Carries the wire-RHR over unchanged — it gives a radial B for a coil, which is wrong
distractor_misconceptions.C: Invents a hand swap instead of a ROLE swap
distractor_misconceptions.D: Inverts which part of the hand tracks which physical quantity

questions.tested_idea: The grip's roles swap: fingers = I (curling), thumb = B (axial)
questions.parallel_form_stem: Looking down a solenoid's axis you see the current circulating counterclockwise. Which way does B inside point?

questions.stem: A 500-turn solenoid of length 0.5 m is stretched to 1.0 m, keeping the same 500 turns and the same current. What happens to B inside?

options.A: B halves, because n = N/L halves
options.B: B is unchanged, because the total turns N are unchanged
options.C: B doubles, because the solenoid is now longer
options.D: B becomes zero, because stretching breaks the field

distractor_misconceptions.B: Believes the raw count N sets the field rather than the density n
distractor_misconceptions.C: Assumes more length means more field instead of more dilution
distractor_misconceptions.D: Treats the long-solenoid formula as fragile rather than density-driven

questions.tested_idea: Only the density n = N/L enters B = μ₀nI
questions.parallel_form_stem: Two solenoids carry the same current and have the same total turns, but one is twice as long. How do their interior fields compare?

questions.stem: The current through a solenoid is reversed. What happens to the field inside?

options.A: B flips to point the opposite way and the N/S pole ends swap; the magnitude |B| = μ₀nI is unchanged
options.B: Both the direction and the magnitude of B change
options.C: Nothing changes — the formula has no minus sign
options.D: The field vanishes while the current settles, then returns radially

distractor_misconceptions.B: Couples the sign flip to an imagined strength change
distractor_misconceptions.C: Reads the magnitude formula as if direction did not exist
distractor_misconceptions.D: Invents a transient and falls back to the radial-field picture

questions.tested_idea: Reversal flips direction and poles only; |B| is unchanged
questions.parallel_form_stem: A freely suspended solenoid has its battery terminals swapped. What does the solenoid do, and what happens to |B| inside?
